parsed_review.summary:
CP00-822 is the RP27 Platform Extensibility "Index export check + Synthetic Fixture Set foundation" descriptor pack (risk A, 10 units, RP27.P01.M05.S22-RP27.P01.M06.S09), following CP00-821 (RP27.P01.M05.S12-S21) and CP00-820 (RP27.P00.M00.S01-RP27.P01.M05.S11). Reviewed read-only via Read/Grep/Glob only; no Bash, no writes.

Production-ready claim is correctly scoped and honest. manifest.json declares production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, status:in_progress, production_ready_flag="platform_extensibility_index_export_fixture_foundation_descriptor_verified", implementation_summary descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, production_ready_descriptor_verified:false. The pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim.

Unit tiling verified exact and non-overlapping. registry.js PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_CP822_PACK_BINDING (CP00-822, risk A, 10 units, RP27.P01.M05.S22-RP27.P01.M06.S09, upstream CP00-821, next CP00-823/RP27.P01.M06.S10, flag ...index_export_fixture_foundation_descriptor_verified) reconciles with the baseline, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, the live closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-822 entry (10 included_units), and the contract current_pack/latest_pack. CP822_REQUIREMENTS deliverable_counts {implementation:8, ui:2}=10, phase {RP27.P01:10}, micro_phase {RP27.P01.M05:1, RP27.P01.M06:9}, micro_title {Permission And Audit Binding:1, Synthetic Fixture Set:9}, and required_section_rows RP27.P01.M05=["Index export check"] (S22) + RP27.P01.M06=DOMAIN_ROWS.slice(0,9) (S01-S09) all match my direct count of the live-plan units. S22 is the 22nd domain row "Index export check" (CP820 took slice 0-11, CP821 took 11-21); M06 opens the Synthetic Fixture Set using the first 9 domain-row templates. Unit IDs are distinct from all prior packs; CP00-823 cleanly begins at RP27.P01.M06.S10.

Descriptor and safety boundaries hold. createPlatformExtensibilityCp822IndexExportFixtureFoundationDescriptor sets source_descriptor=CP821, authority_boundary claude_is_final_approval:false / local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false / human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true / runtime_opening_pack_id:CP00-823, spreads NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, hermes_packet emits_runtime_receipt:false, claude_packet read_only:true + promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, closeout_handoff to CP00-823/RP27.P01.M06.S10. createRow (registry.js 333-352) hardcodes descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false and every *_secret/*_included/*_issued/*_delivered/*_executed/*_written flag false, spreads NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, and deepFreezes each row. The serialized contract projections.cp822 rows (M05 index_export_check; M06 package_directory_layout...optional_field_registry) confirm all flags false; validation.valid:true, latest_pack_id/plan_pack_id CP00-822, no_write_attestation all false, 6 no_leak_guards, 9 mandatory_artifacts, 5 safety_gates. model.js models are all tenant_scoped:true, require matter_trace_ref (commonRequiredFields), and reference AuditEvent; states.js EXTENSION_RISK_CLAIMS has 6 entries. No API keys, secrets, private keys, passwords, or real emails found in the contract or platform package.

Tests and harness wired correctly. model.test.js adds three CP822 tests (binding+coverage with by_deliverable deepEqual and by_micro_phase RP27.P01.M06=9; descriptor runtime-closed with api_key_issued/webhook_delivered/workflow_executed=false, source_descriptor=CP821, handoff to next_pack_id; contract projection keeps CP00-822 current via latest_pack/projections.cp822/latest_projection/validation.latest_pack_id). The CP820/CP821 "keeps current" tests assert only historical membership + projection descriptor, so they survive CP822 superseding them. All CP822 exports flow through index.js (export *). scripts/validate-rp27-platform-extensibility-contract.mjs is retargeted to CP822 and validates coverage+descriptor+contract+capabilities+gates with process.exit(1) on any error.

Authority and CP178+ hardening boundaries hold. Claude stays read_only_independent_reviewer (tools Read,Grep,Glob; claude_is_final_approval:false; promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false); the human/enterprise-trust approval boundary is external to this receipt and the pack does not claim it (per prompt, this does not set blocks_goal_closeout). Hermes is control-plane review-packet/receipt handling only (emits_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1434-byte stdout, valid JSON {"ok":true}, no stderr); validation-report confirms summary_only_review_allowed:true, prompt_contains_full_diff_or_source:false, source_inspection_mode read_tools_used, forbidden_tools_present:false, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted:[]; forbidden_review_evidence is defined. No prior review receipt exists (this is the single pending review).

Provenance: all PASS judgments are from direct source inspection. As a read-only reviewer I did NOT execute npm test / npm run closeout-pack:validate / validate / spec:requirements:validate / weighted:validate / fullplan:validate / goal:closeout:validate / rp00:control-plane:validate / node scripts/validate-rp27-platform-extensibility-contract.mjs / git diff --check; command/test PASS is established by cross-source consistency (registry/validators/model.test/contract/live-plan all reconcile; contract validation.valid:true) and CP821 precedent, not fresh execution. Closeout artifacts (command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present - the expected pre-closeout state, matching CP00-821 before its close. Four P3 notes only; no P0/P1/P2. The persisting cosmetic changed-file-scope.json dual-listing of docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md (in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved) and the README.md current-pack wording still naming CP00-820 (README already lists all 6 risks) are non-blocking carryovers and do not affect the authoritative active_pack_implementation_refs (the 5 platform files). None of these block pack closeout, and the external human/enterprise-trust approval boundary does not block this CP closeout goal.

findings.title: Closeout artifacts not yet present at review time; repo-wide validation commands not independently executed (read-only)
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts (line 225) lists manifest.json, command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but docs/closeout-packs/cp00-822/ currently contains only manifest.json, and pack_level_claude_review.status (line 220) is 'pending_review' with empty valid_review_receipts/invalid_review_attempts. This is the expected pre-closeout state: this review is the gate that precedes generating command-evidence (validation command results), claude-review-result, construction-inspection, and adjudication, then flipping production_ready/runtime status - exactly as the prior closed pack CP00-821 demonstrates (it now has all five docs artifacts plus raw-output.json/review-receipt.json). As a read-only reviewer I could not run npm run closeout-pack:validate / npm test / npm run validate / spec:requirements / weighted / fullplan / goal:closeout / rp00:control-plane validators / node scripts/validate-rp27-platform-extensibility-contract.mjs / git diff --check, so command/test PASS is established by cross-source consistency (registry/validators/model.test/contract/live-plan internally aligned, contract validation.valid:true) and CP821 precedent rather than fresh execution.
findings.recommendation: Proceed. At closeout, record exactly one valid pack-level review receipt for this run, then generate command-evidence.json (confirm npm test and the listed validators, including node scripts/validate-rp27-platform-extensibility-contract.mjs, actually exit 0 on the CP00-822 tree), construction-inspection.json, adjudication.md, and claude-review-result.json before flipping status/production_ready.

findings.title: CP822 descriptor validator omits two defense-in-depth row checks present in the CP820 validator
findings.details: validatePlatformExtensibilityCp822IndexExportFixtureFoundationDescriptor (lines 280-322) checks each row for descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, and real_client_data_included/permission_decision_detail_included/audit_event_body_included:false, but (like the CP821 validator) drops two checks the CP820 validator still has: (a) the api_key_secret_included/webhook_secret_included/workflow_payload_included row check (CP820 validators.js lines 117-119) and (b) the rows-level api_key_issued/webhook_delivered/workflow_executed runtime-open assertion (CP820 lines 125-128). No unsafe data passes: createRow (registry.js lines 333-352) hardcodes all of those flags false, spreads NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, and deepFreezes every row; the serialized contract projections.cp822 rows confirm all flags false; and the CP822 descriptor test asserts descriptor.api_key_issued/webhook_delivered/workflow_executed===false. So this is a validator-parity/defense-in-depth gap (the validator is slightly less strict than CP820 against hypothetical row drift), not an exploitable leak. Same carryover as CP821-R02; the prior recommendation was not adopted.
findings.recommendation: Optionally add the two assertions for parity with the CP820 validator: reject any row with api_key_secret_included/webhook_secret_included/workflow_payload_included !== false, and reject any row with api_key_issued/webhook_delivered/workflow_executed === true. No functional change to current safe data.

findings.title: Unused import PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_CP821_PACK_BINDING in the retargeted validate script
findings.details: The script imports PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_CP821_PACK_BINDING (line 5) but never references it in the body (lines 17-58), which exclusively use the CP822 binding/requirements and CP822 validators. It appears to be a leftover from retargeting the script from CP821 to CP822. Cosmetic dead code only - ES modules do not error on unused imports and no lint gate fails on it; the script still validates CP822 coverage/descriptor/contract/capabilities/gates and process.exit(1) on any error. No functional or boundary impact.
findings.recommendation: Optionally remove the unused PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_CP821_PACK_BINDING import, or put it to use as an explicit upstream-pack continuity assertion (e.g., assert CP822 binding.upstream_pack_id === CP821 binding.pack_id). No behavior change either way.

findings.title: program_contract.acceptance_risks lists 5 risks while blocked_claims/EXTENSION_RISK_CLAIMS/README treat cross-tenant extension access as a 6th
findings.details: program_contract.acceptance_risks (contract lines 30-36; also registry.js PLATFORM_EXTENSIBILITY_PROGRAM_CONTRACT.acceptance_risks line 32 and the cp822 projection) lists 5 risks: API over-permission, webhook replay, workflow unsafe mutation, rate limit bypass, extension leak. states.js EXTENSION_RISK_CLAIMS, the descriptor blocked_claims, and packages/platform/README.md (lines 9-14) all list 6, adding cross_tenant_extension_access. The risk is fully covered elsewhere (blocked_claims superset of 6, safety_gate 'enforce tenant scope', per-row tenant_scope_field cross_tenant_extension_access_allowed:false, model registry tenant_scoped:true), so this is a descriptive count inconsistency, not a coverage gap, and is the same carryover flagged as CP820-R03 / CP821-R04 - unchanged by this pack.
findings.recommendation: Optionally align program_contract.acceptance_risks with blocked_claims (add 'cross-tenant extension access') or annotate that blocked_claims is intentionally a defense-in-depth superset; no functional or boundary impact.
